    "resolution": "backport",

        "package": "glib2",
        "patch_url": "https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/glib/-/commit/b94b44407a088e6e8278d9db8b59fb377e84bda4.patch",
        "justification": "The patch fixes a serial number overflow issue in GDBusConnection by checking if the serial counter is at its maximum value. *Resolution*: backport
*Patch URL*: https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/glib/-/commit/b94b44407a088e6e8278d9db8b59fb377e84bda4.patch
*Justification*: The patch fixes a serial number overflow issue in GDBusConnection by checking if the serial counter is at its maximum value. If it is, the patch resets the counter to 0, which after incrementing becomes 1, effectively preventing the serial number from becoming 0 and causing a crash.
*Fix Version*: rhel-9.7.z

components.description: A library of handy utility functions
components.name: glib2

fields.description:
This is a clone of issue RHEL-4139 to use for version rhel-9.7.z
--
Original description:
Description of problem:

If Gnome terminal kept running for many days / months it is getting crashed and resulted in segfault. 

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

gnome-terminal-3.28.2-3.el7.x86_64

How reproducible:

Frequently

Steps to Reproduce:
1.Start the gnome-terminal
2.Run the command / application like "TOP","HTOP" for many weeks / months.
3.Suddenly the gnome-terminal crashes

Actual results:

Gnome-terminal is crashing if kept running / used for longer period of time.

Expected results:

Gnome-terminal should be able to run continuously without any issue. 

Additional info:
